description: Native strategy family research map for rates and FX expansion
context: Cross-asset transfer test showed 0/50 direct transfers from equity families. These asset classes require purpose-built strategy logic.

families.name: Intraday Rate Mean Reversion
families.concept: Rates are strongly mean-reverting intraday. Fade extreme moves from session VWAP or rolling mean, exit at reversion to mean.
families.rationale: Rates intraday microstructure favors mean reversion due to dealer inventory management and hedging flows

families.name: Daily-Bar Rate Momentum
families.concept: Rates trend strongly on daily+ timeframes driven by macro expectations. Use daily bars with trend-following logic (EMA crossover, breakout).
families.rationale: Rate trends driven by Fed policy expectations, inflation data. Multi-day holding captures macro trends that intraday misses entirely.

families.name: Yield Curve Spread Trade
families.concept: Trade ZN vs ZB (or ZF vs ZN) spread based on curve steepening/flattening signals.
indicators: Spread mean reversion from historical range
families.rationale: Yield curve slope is a fundamental macro signal. Spread trades are market-neutral (low beta to rates level).

families.name: Macro Event Response
families.concept: Trade systematic patterns around FOMC, CPI, NFP, PPI releases. Rates have well-documented pre/post event behavior.
families.rationale: Rates are the most macro-sensitive asset class. Event patterns are structural and well-documented in academic literature.

families.name: Session Transition Fade
families.concept: Fade the opening move at London or NY open. Rates often overshoot at session transitions as new participants enter.
families.session: London open (03:00 ET), NY open (08:00 ET)
families.rationale: 23-hour market means session transitions create temporary dislocations that mean-revert.

families.name: London/NY Session Breakout
families.concept: FX volatility clusters around London (03:00-04:00 ET) and NY (08:00-09:00 ET) opens. Trade breakouts from pre-session consolidation ranges.
families.rationale: FX session breakout is one of the most well-documented intraday FX edges. London open accounts for majority of daily range.

families.name: Daily-Bar FX Trend Following
families.concept: FX pairs trend well on daily timeframe. Simple trend-following (EMA, Donchian) on daily bars.
families.rationale: FX daily trend following has decades of academic evidence (carry + momentum factors). Different alpha source from intraday.

families.name: Carry-Biased Direction
families.concept: Use interest rate differential as directional bias filter. Go long high-yielding currencies, short low-yielding when trend confirms.
families.rationale: Carry trade is a well-known risk premium in FX. Using it as a filter rather than standalone reduces drawdown.

families.name: Cross-Pair Momentum
families.concept: If EUR/USD and GBP/USD both trending up, the shared USD weakness signal is stronger. Use cross-pair confirmation for higher-conviction entries.
families.rationale: Cross-pair analysis provides independent confirmation that reduces false signals.

families.name: Intraday FX Mean Reversion
families.concept: Fade extreme deviations from session VWAP in quiet periods (late US, early Asian session).
families.rationale: FX mean reversion works in specific low-vol windows. Lower priority than session breakout.

recommended_first_3_prototypes.name: London/NY Session Breakout
recommended_first_3_prototypes.reason: Most documented FX intraday edge. Infrastructure already supports ORB-style logic — adapt session times and range definitions.

recommended_first_3_prototypes.name: Intraday Rate Mean Reversion
recommended_first_3_prototypes.reason: Rates are structurally mean-reverting intraday. VWAP deviation logic exists in FQL — adapt for rates session/vol characteristics.

recommended_first_3_prototypes.name: Daily-Bar FX Trend Following
recommended_first_3_prototypes.reason: Opens multi-horizon lane (daily bars). Decades of academic evidence. Genuinely uncorrelated to intraday equity strategies.
